expectations.text: Correctly warns that Azure Key Vault converts underscores to hyphens (DATABASE_URL becomes DATABASE-URL)
expectations.evidence: "Azure Key Vault does not allow underscores in secret names. When you sync secrets from Infisical to Azure Key Vault, all underscores are automatically converted to hyphens. Example mappings: DATABASE_URL → DATABASE-URL, API_KEY → API-KEY"

expectations.text: Lists required permissions: secrets/list, secrets/get, secrets/set, and secrets/recover
expectations.evidence: Table clearly lists all four permissions: 'secrets/list' (check what exists), 'secrets/get' (read values), 'secrets/set' (create/update), 'secrets/recover' (recover soft-deleted)'

expectations.text: Recommends Key Vault Secrets Officer role as the appropriate built-in role
expectations.evidence: "Recommended Role: Key Vault Secrets Officer — This built-in Azure role includes all the permissions above and is specifically designed for secret management operations."

expectations.text: Explains why secrets/recover is needed (Azure soft-delete behavior)
expectations.evidence: "Why secrets/recover Matters: Azure Key Vault uses soft-delete by default, meaning deleted secrets remain recoverable for a retention period. If Infisical attempts to update a secret that was recently deleted, it needs the secrets/recover permission to restore it first before updating."
"Create an Azure App Connection in Infisical with your service principal credentials"

expectations.text: Mentions the vault base URL as a required destination config field
expectations.evidence: "Destination: Select your Key Vault's base URL (e.g., https://my-vault.vault.azure.net)"
